name: Taylor expansion exact solution formula
subsection_title: The Taylor expansion of the exact solution
statement_text:
Let S denote a finite ordered set. Then
                                  
                   y (#S) (y0 ) =      F (|t|)(y0 ).
                                  t∈TS
statement_latex:
\begin{theorem}[311B]
Let $S$ denote a finite ordered set. Then
\[
y^{(\#S)}(y_0) = \sum_{t \in T_S} F(|t|)(y_0).
\]
\end{theorem}
proof_text: Proof. In the case |t| = τ , the result is obvious. For the case #S > 1, apply
context_latex:
\paragraph{Context}
Let $S$ be a finite ordered set and $T_S$ the set of trees derived from $S$. For $t \in T_S$, let $|t|$ denote its symmetry and $F(|t|)$ the associated elementary differential. Let $y(x)$ be the solution function with initial value $y_0 = y(x_0)$.

variables.name: TS
variables.definition: set of trees derived from S (defined in preceding context)

variables.name: |t|
variables.definition: symmetry of tree t (defined in preceding context)

variables.name: F(|t|)
variables.definition: elementary differential associated with tree t (defined in preceding context)

preamble: The theorem relates the (#S)-th derivative of the solution y at y0 to a sum over trees in TS of elementary differentials F(|t|) evaluated at y0. The preceding text discusses differentiation of elementary differentials and combinatorial structures of trees labelled by S.
